residual_risks: components/Layout.js now loads Space_Grotesk via next/font/google at build time. This adds a runtime dependency on network access to Google's font CDN during `next build`. On most Vercel builds this is a non-issue, but if the diff is ever built in a restricted-network CI environment (air-gapped runner, offline SSG rebuild), the build will fail outright with no code-level fallback (no 'local' or system-font fallback specified in fontFamily.display).

styles/globals.css adds `body::before { position: fixed; ... z-index: -1 }` to render the new pastel gradient. Browsers differ in how `position: fixed` elements are handled in print media (some repeat the layer per printed page, others render it once or drop it). Printed/PDF-exported blog posts may lose the gradient background inconsistently across browsers -- purely cosmetic, no functional impact.

Card (`bg-white/80`) and Nav (`bg-white/50`) are now translucent surfaces layered over a fixed 3-stop gradient (blue -> lavender -> cyan). Content that renders outside a Card (e.g. any future MDX block, image, or code sample placed directly against the page background rather than inside Card) will sit directly on the gradient; contrast/readability of dark indigo text (#1e1b4b) against the lightest gradient stop hasn't been visually verified beyond the reviewed diff.
testing_gaps: No visual regression or screenshot test exists to confirm the fixed gradient background, translucent Card/Nav layering, and Space Grotesk heading font render correctly across breakpoints and in print media -- this is a pure-CSS/JSX change with no automated visual coverage.

No CI step verifies `next build` succeeds with the new Google Fonts fetch in the actual deploy environment; the only signal is the presence of vercel.json, which wasn't exercised as part of this review.
